body: my theory is that you can tell how long someone has lived here by how little they react when the tube says severe delays
paid 4.20 for coffee before work and had that disgusting flash of relief like i had somehow beaten london this morning
heard "i'm not mad i'm just freezing" on the victoria line and it was more emotionally mature than most conversations i've had this month
the weird thing about london is people will apologise for touching your sleeve on the overground and then send you a rent increase like it's weather
i still choose my tube carriage based on the exit i want and somehow still manage to come up behind a family with three suitcases every single time
nothing exposes a person faster in london than how confidently they call a 17 minute walk "basically next door"
woman in the corner shop saw i was buying paracetamol, crisps, and a lucozade and gave me the little nod reserved for people clearly losing to the week
people say london is romantic in the rain but most of that romance disappears the second one trouser leg stays wet until lunch
the pub carpet smell was still hanging in the stairwell at 8am and somehow that told me more about the building than the letting agent ever did
i have a rule in london: if a coffee queue needs barriers, you've built a lifestyle brand, not a cafe
on the night bus home a guy told his friend "we don't need closure, we need kebabs" and i genuinely think that was wisdom
the only way to stay sane here is to treat every overground delay like a personal insult you are too tired to process properly
guy at the spati saw my face and handed me the coldest bottle from the back fridge without me asking. most emotionally intelligent interaction i've had all week
my rule is that if a place still lets people stand outside holding one beer for forty minutes without making it an identity, it hasn't fully died yet
i still rehearse one german sentence before the post office like i'm about to defend a thesis and then immediately get asked something else
the weird thing about berlin is everyone performs freedom right up until you ask them to reply to a message or show up on time
ringbahn delay this morning and one entire carriage somehow looked dressed for three different emotional climates at once
it took me too long to realise the most local thing you can do in berlin is stop narrating yourself so much
woman at the bakery corrected my article, switched to english out of pity, then looked annoyed that i answered in english. efficient humiliation
nothing says berlin rent discourse like a new cafe where the stools look temporary but the prices sound permanent
someone on the u8 said "i don't need more space, i need lower expectations" and honestly that covered rent, dating, and bureaucracy in one go
the best version of sunday closure is when the whole city goes quiet enough that the spati fridge suddenly sounds philosophical
i have a rule in berlin: if a person says they know a secret canal route, they are either useful or recently heartbroken
the guy in our wg kitchen labelled one shelf "temporary politics" and somehow that explained the entire flat better than any group chat
the weird thing about sf is everyone says they want community and then schedules friendship like a dentist appointment three weeks out
by 9:12 i had three calendar invites, one "quick question" in slack, and a wellness app reminding me to breathe. fully unserious city
my theory is that sf coffee shops are mostly places where people go to be seen surviving their own ambition in public
muni delay this morning and the guy next to me kept refreshing his startup dashboard like the train personally owed his seed round something
nothing exposes a person faster here than saying they love cities and then acting personally betrayed by muni existing
a corner store cat asleep under electrolyte drinks did more for my blood pressure today than every wellness product in soma
people talk about sf like the fog makes everything poetic. mostly it just gives you two wrong outfits and one expensive coffee decision before noon
heard "we're not behind, we're pre-revenue" on the bus and it explained more about this city than most local journalism
it took me too long to realise the most stable relationship in san francisco is between people and the version of themselves they are about to become
tourists stop in the middle of the sidewalk to decide if a burrito line means authenticity. locals just count the construction vests and commit
i have a rule in sf: if someone mentions their startup before their neighborhood, i assume they don't actually live anywhere emotionally
the bart escalator stalled and for one perfect second everyone in the station looked like they had the same therapist
someone stopped in the bike lane in gracia to film a doorway and three locals swerved around him like silent mutual contempt is just another transport layer here
the real sign a brunch place is not for locals isn't the menu, it's whether anyone inside looks remotely rushed
same tiny coffee, same old man already arguing about football, same five minutes where i can pretend i belong before i have to translate the day again
people say barcelona is effortless but half the city runs on carefully managed lateness and pretending the rent conversation can wait another month
metro line 3 was so hot this morning that even the tourists looked briefly local
my theory is that suitcase wheels on old pavement are the actual soundtrack of barcelona becoming less for the people who live here
i ordered in catalan with rehearsed confidence, got answered in spanish, panicked, and finished in english. unbelievable display from everyone involved
nothing exposes a person faster here than how they describe gracia after one weekend and a single vermut
an old man started arguing about football before noon and the whole square relaxed like someone had restored the correct operating system
the only way to stay sane here is to know which bakery still feels like bread and which one now feels like content
heard "it's only temporary" on an el born staircase and genuinely couldn't tell if they meant the apartment, the relationship, or the city
the weird thing about barcelona is that sunlight keeps talking you into emotional optimism your bank account has not approved
the real sign someone lives in london isn't oyster confidence, it's knowing which side of the platform makes the victoria line exit feel less humiliating
every table in this cafe was laptops, strollers, and one man correcting someone's german, which felt like the whole city printed in three fonts
the line between ambitious and spiritually overcaffeinated is only visible in san francisco once the second six dollar coffee arrives
another tiny cafe switched from cheap coffee to polished matcha and everyone on the block somehow heard about it before lunch
